message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="kent-beck" color="blue" summary="Status codes already correct in agreements, no change needed">
Thanks for the detailed explanation, Carson. The good news is the agreements already have this right. Line 283-284 reads:

> "Status codes: 200 for success with HTML, 422 for validation errors with error HTML, 200 with empty body for deletion (allows HTMX swap to remove element)."

No 204 in sight. Your clarification about WHY (HTMX skips the swap on 204) is valuable context for the team when we're implementing handlers. That's exactly the kind of detail the mob will benefit from hearing when we write our first DELETE endpoint.

The agreements are finalized with consensus. We're all set.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- Kent confirmed the status codes were already correct in the final document. No action needed on my end. The team agreements are locked in and we're ready to move forward.

input.content:
Perfect. Glad the document already had it right. And yes -- when we're in the mob writing that first DELETE handler, I'll make sure to explain the "why" behind the 200-empty-body convention. The whole point of mobbing is that everyone understands what the code is doing and why. No cargo culting status codes.

All set on my end. Standing by for the walking skeleton.
input.summary: Confirmed, standing by for walking skeleton work

routing.summary: Confirmed, standing by for walking skeleton work

content.text: All wrapped up. Team agreements are finalized with full consensus, status codes are correct as written, and I'm standing by for the walking skeleton phase. Nothing left to do until the next task comes in.
